title: Đề luyện tập 04 - RISC-V, Cache và Pipeline
description: Đề số 04 gồm 10 câu mới. Mỗi câu 10 điểm, tổng 100 điểm. Đáp án chỉ hiện sau khi nộp bài.

questions.title: Cache parameters: 4-way set associative, write-back
questions.prompt:
Here is the cache with the following key characteristics:
■ 4-Way Set Associative Cache
■ Write policy: Write-back using write allocate with one Dirty bit per block
■ Block size is 64 bytes
■ Cache size is 64 KB
■ Memory is byte addressing with 32-bit addresses

fields.label: Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: Blocks = 64KB/64B = 1024. Sets = 1024/4 = 256, so index = 8 bits. Offset = log2(64)=6 bits. Tag = 32-8-6=18 bits. Total = 1024×(512+18+1 valid+1 dirty)=544768 bits.

questions.title: Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: sw
questions.prompt:
Consider a single-cycle RISC-V processor with add, sub, and, or, lw, sw and beq.

Let the processor execute:
sw x9, -48(x14)
at address 3000. Suppose register xi = 24i for i=1,...,31. Suppose memory[M] = M + 700.
questions.solution: For sw, RegWrite=0, MemWrite=1, ALUSrc=1. x14=336 and x9=216. Effective address = 336-48=288. The data memory read output is unknown (?) and the write-back value is x.

questions.title: Fully associative cache with true LRU
questions.prompt:
Assume the main memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a fully associative cache with two-word blocks and a total size of 16 words. Assume one word is 32 bits.

Sequence of word addresses:
0x2, 0x70, 0x1d, 0x3, 0x72, 0x40, 0x1c, 0x90, 0x41, 0x71, 0x92, 0x2

Assume a true LRU replacement policy. For each reference, identify binary word address, tag, and hit/miss.

fields_intro.label: Number of blocks in the cache
Number of sets in the cache
Number of blocks in the memory
Number of tag bits in each block
Total bits required to implement the cache

fields_extra.label: After these accesses, is the cache full?

questions.solution: Block offset = 1 bit(s), set index = 0 bit(s), tag = 7 bit(s). Hit/Miss sequence: M, M, M, H, M, M, H, M, H, H, M, H. Cache full: no. Replacements: 0.

questions.title: Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: sub
questions.prompt:
Let the processor execute:
sub x20, x13, x6
at address 3004. Suppose register xi = 24i for i=1,...,31. Suppose memory[M] = M + 700.
questions.solution: R-type sub uses ALUSrc=0, ALUOp=10, ALU control O=0110. x13=312, x6=144, so ALU result = 168 and this value is written to x20.

questions.title: 3-way set associative cache, điền Way theo từng dòng
questions.prompt:
Assume the memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a 3-way set associative cache with 2-word blocks and a total size of 48 words. Assume 1 word = 64 bits.

First, fill in the cache parameters. Then use true LRU. For each reference, identify binary address, tag, set index, offset, hit/miss, and the tags in each way after the reference has been handled.

Lưu ý: Nếu một Way có nhiều dòng như T(001)=0000, T(010)=1011, mỗi dòng sẽ là một ô nhập riêng.

Sequence of word addresses:
0x8, 0x96, 0x39, 0x9, 0x9e, 0x70, 0x9f, 0x1e, 0x5f, 0x97, 0x9f, 0xb8, 0x3f, 0xfe

fields_intro.label: Number of blocks in the cache
Number of sets in the cache
Number of blocks in the memory
Number of tag bits in each block
Total bits required to implement the cache

fields_extra.label: After these accesses, is the cache full?

questions.solution: Block offset = 1 bit(s), set index = 3 bit(s), tag = 4 bit(s). Hit/Miss sequence: M, M, M, H, M, M, H, M, M, H, H, M, M, M. Cache full: no. Replacements: 2.

questions.title: 5-stage pipeline with forwarding and hazard detection
questions.prompt:
Assume that the following code sequence is executed on the 5-stage pipelined processor with forwarding and hazard detection units shown below:
add x18, x7,  x8
lw  x19, 8(x18)
lw  x20, 0(x18)
or  x19, x18, x19
sw  x19, 4(x18)
For the first seven cycles, specify the values of control signals at A, B, C, D, E, F, and G.
questions.solution: This sequence has the same forwarding pattern as add → lw → lw → or → sw. A and B stay 1 for the first seven cycles. Forwarding selections are C/D = 10/00, 01/00, 00/01, 00/10 in cycles 4 to 7.

questions.title: Cache parameters: 2-way set associative, write-back
questions.prompt:
Here is the cache with the following key characteristics:
■ 2-Way Set Associative Cache
■ Write policy: Write-back using write allocate with one Dirty bit per block
■ Block size is 32 bytes
■ Cache size is 16 KB
■ Memory is byte addressing with 32-bit addresses

fields.label: Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: Blocks = 16KB/32B = 512. Sets = 512/2 = 256, so index = 8 bits. Offset = 5 bits. Tag = 19 bits. Total = 512×(256+19+1+1)=141824 bits.

questions.title: Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: lw
questions.prompt:
Let the processor execute:
lw x12, 160(x5)
at address 4096. Suppose register xi = 24i for i=1,...,31. Suppose memory[M] = M + 700.
questions.solution: x5=120. Effective address = 120+160=280. Memory[280]=980, so N=980 is written to x12.

questions.title: Execution time: single-cycle vs pipeline
questions.prompt:
Consider the following sequence of 32-bit RISC-V instructions:
lw  x5, 0(x1)
add x6, x5, x2
sub x7, x6, x3
or  x8, x7, x4
sw  x8, 4(x1)
a) Single-cycle processor clock cycle time = 1000 ps.
b) 5-stage pipelined processor clock cycle time = 250 ps, without forwarding or hazard detection units. Insert NOPs to ensure correct execution.
c) 5-stage pipelined processor clock cycle time = 250 ps, with forwarding/hazard detection units.
d) Which case gives the medium performance?

fields.label: a) Execution time on single-cycle processor
b) Number of cycles after inserting NOPs
b) Execution time without forwarding/hazard detection
c) Number of stalls/NOPs needed with forwarding/hazard detection
c) Execution time with forwarding/hazard detection

questions.solution: Single-cycle time = 5000 ps. Without forwarding/hazard detection: 8 NOPs, cycles = 17, time = 4250 ps. With forwarding/hazard detection: 1 stall(s), cycles = 10, time = 2500 ps. Medium performance is case b.

questions.title: 2-way set associative cache with true LRU
questions.prompt:
Assume the main memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a 2-way set associative cache with 1-word blocks and a total size of 8 words. Assume one word is 32 bits.

Sequence of word addresses:
0x1, 0x9, 0x11, 0x19, 0x1, 0x21, 0x9, 0x29, 0x31, 0x11, 0x39, 0x1

Assume a true LRU replacement policy. For each reference, identify binary address, tag, set index, offset, and hit/miss.

fields_intro.label: Number of blocks in the cache
Number of sets in the cache
Number of blocks in the memory
Number of tag bits in each block
Total bits required to implement the cache

fields_extra.label: After these accesses, is the cache full?

questions.solution: Block offset = 0 bit(s), set index = 2 bit(s), tag = 6 bit(s). Hit/Miss sequence: M, M, M, M, M, M, M, M, M, M, M, M. Cache full: no. Replacements: 10.
